taxonomy: Saguaster vitiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Staphylocarpidae, but it is more commonly classified as a species of Aglaia, a genus of flowering plants in the family Meliaceae. This classification is based on various studies and taxonomic analyses. Aglaia vitiensis is a tropical plant native to the Pacific Island of Fiji, which is also known as Saguaster vitiensis in some sources. benefits: Saguaster vitiensis, also known as the Fijian breadfruit, has been a staple food source in the Pacific Islands for centuries.
